rxn_class_summary: PPTS-promoted divergent conversion of a grayanane-type intermediate 11 into 1-epi-33 and 33 via competing Grob fragmentation/carbonyl-ene and E1 elimination pathways, applied to total syntheses of grayanoids

key_intermediates.name: INT1, hydrogen-bonded complex of 11 with PPTS (pyridinium tosylate and acetate counterion network)
INT2, ion-paired oxocarbenium/carbocationic intermediate after C-OAc heterolysis via TS1
INT3, higher-energy isomeric ion pair in equilibrium with INT2, leading to E1 elimination
10A, macrocyclic Grob fragmentation product bearing Δ1,10-alkene
10B, reactive conformer of 10A leading to 1-epi-33 via TS4
10C, reactive conformer of 10A leading to 5-epi-33 via TS5 (disfavored)
33, E1 elimination/Prins-type product from INT3 via TS3

electron_flow: PPTS hydrogen-bonds to 11 (INT1) and promotes heterolytic C1-OAc cleavage through TS1 to give the ion pair INT2; INT2 either undergoes deprotonation-triggered Grob fragmentation of the C1-C5 bond (TS2) to macrocycle 10A, which then collapses by concerted transannular carbonyl-ene cyclization (TS4) to 1-epi-33, or isomerizes to INT3 and undergoes E1 elimination (TS3) to give 33.
rate_determining_step: Either Grob fragmentation via TS2 (ΔG‡ = 35.9 kcal/mol) for the 1-epi-33 pathway or E1 elimination via TS3 (ΔG‡ = 37.7 kcal/mol) for the 33 pathway; the close barriers rationalize the observed ~2.5:1 dr

probe_reagents.name: BF3·Et2O (Lewis acid screen)
probe_reagents.observation: Substantial decomposition of 11 at 0 °C in CH2Cl2

probe_reagents.name: TsOH·H2O
probe_reagents.observation: <5% of both 1-epi-33 and 33 in toluene at 110 °C

probe_reagents.name: PPTS (pyridinium p-toluenesulfonate)
probe_reagents.observation: Best conditions: 2.5:1 1-epi-33:33 in 80% combined yield at 110 °C, toluene

probe_reagents.name: Py·HCl
probe_reagents.observation: Diminished yield (18% of 33), supporting role of tosylate counterion

probe_reagents.name: 2,6-lutidine·TsOH
probe_reagents.observation: Low yields (9% 1-epi-33, 20% 33); ortho-substitution on pyridinium disrupts H-bond network

probe_reagents.name: para-substituted pyridinium tosylate variants (Table S2 entries 25, 26)
probe_reagents.observation: Electron-deficient pyridinium tunes acetate basicity and biases pathway selectivity, validating the H-bond-weaved counterion model

probe_reagents.name: DFT (ωB97X-V-SMD(toluene)/def2-TZVPP//B3LYP-D4-CPCM(toluene)/def2-SVP)
probe_reagents.observation: Located INT1/INT2/INT3 and TS1-TS5; predicts ΔG‡(TS2)=35.9 vs ΔG‡(TS3)=37.7 kcal/mol and ΔΔG‡(TS5-TS4)=4.0 kcal/mol explaining absence of 5-epi-33

notes: Alternative pathway considered: direct PPTS-promoted Prins cyclization of 10A to 33 was computed to be kinetically unfavorable (ΔG‡ = 37.7 kcal/mol, Figure S3), so 33 is assigned exclusively to the E1 elimination route from INT3. The diastereoselectivity of the transannular carbonyl-ene step is rationalized by an eclipsed (disfavored, TS5, leads to 5-epi-33) vs staggered (favored, TS4, leads to 1-epi-33) transition state with torsional strain developing as the C6-H/C5-O dihedral rotates from -23.1° to +22.1°. The hydrogen-bond-weaved PPTS-acetate counterion is invoked as the key organizing element enabling pathway divergence.
